name: Bavarian Pot Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven or large pan with a tight-fitting cover, brown the meat in fat. Season with salt and pepper, then remove from the pan and set aside. Pour off the fat drippings.
2. Add apple juice, tomato sauce, onion, ginger, cinnamon, salt, vinegar, and bay leaf to the pan. Mix well.
3. Return the meat to the pan. Cover and simmer for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until tender. Alternatively, cook in a preheated oven at 325°F for the same amount of time.
4. Turn the meat once during cooking to ensure it is evenly cooked throughout. When done, remove the meat and keep warm. Discard the bay leaf.
5. To make gravy, pour the liquid from the pan into a measuring cup. Let stand for 1 minute to allow fat to rise to the top. Remove all but 4 tablespoons (or less) of fat. Add enough water or other liquid to measure 1.5 cups of liquid. Return to the pan.
6. In a separate container, mix 1/2 cup cold water with flour. Gradually add this mixture to the liquid in the pan while stirring constantly. Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, about 3 minutes. Taste the gravy and adjust seasoning if necessary with salt and pepper.
7. Slice the meat and serve the gravy separately.
